meta.title: Chip
meta.description: Compact selection controls — Filter Chip for filtering, Severity Chip for level-based selection.

hero.title: Chips
hero.desc: Chips let users input information or pick a value. A single Chip can act as On/Off; when multiple Chips appear together, they default to single-select.

components.title: Components
components.desc: Selecting a Chip immediately reflects the action and surfaces the resulting data. Basic Filter Chips and Severity Chips filter when there are at least two options. Filter Chips share one selected-state color across labels; Severity Chips differentiate by severity level — each tier maps to its own color.

filter.title: Filter Chip
filter.desc: Filter Chip labels are configurable. In Wind, Filter Chips appear inside a Filter Bar. Each Chip Group's Label Text marks a distinct filter, and all Filter Chips use the same Selected-state color.

state.title: State
state.desc: Basic Filter Chips split by Selected/Unselected into Default, Hover, Selected, Disabled. Selected is treated as the Active state.

size.title: Size
size.desc: Width flexes; height uses 32px (Medium) or 20px (Small). Font size, icon size, radius, and padding vary per size. Heights below 20 (Small) are avoided — they fall below the minimum touch target.

color.title: Color
color.desc: When Active, Filter Chips inherit their category color.

textByte.title: Text Byte
textByte.desc: Each chip caps at 10 characters (22 bytes, including spaces). Beyond that, truncate with an ellipsis and surface the full text in a Tooltip.

placement.title: Placement
placement.desc: Filter Chips inside a Filter Bar default to left-aligned, with two-line layouts allowed. Horizontal margins lock at 16. Label-to-chip gap is 8. Group-to-group horizontal spacing is 32; row spacing is 16.

severity.title: Severity Chip
severity.desc: Severity Chip lets users pick a level for status or defect severity. Each Chip's numeric value represents a fixed Severity Level, and the user picks one value within the defined range. Unlike a typical text-based Chip, Severity Chips deliver a number-centered selection experience within a consistent size and structure. The selected value gets visual emphasis, and a level-based color scheme can apply to make the current severity readable at a glance.

state.title: State
state.desc: Basic Filter Chips split by Selected/Unselected into Default, Hover, Selected, Disabled. Selected is treated as the Active state.

size.title: Size
size.desc:
Width flexes and can be adjusted by situation. Color, severity order, and the number of visible chips shift with the customer's severity policy; chip width flexes by the available area. Default width inside the OSD Viewer: {(container width) − 12 × (total severity count − 1)} / (total severity count). Inside a Modal: {(container width) − 8 × (total severity count − 1)} / (total severity count).

Minimum chip width should not exceed the chip height. When the group overflows the container, fitting within the container takes priority.

color.title: Color
color.desc: When Active, Severity Filter Chips inherit their category color. Severity levels can differ by customer; apply colors starting from the extremes (1 and 5) and work inward.

textByte.title: Text Byte
textByte.desc: Severity Chips use the assigned severity number — typically up to 2 digits.

placement.title: Placement
placement.desc: Default left-aligned, with two-line layouts allowed. Label-to-chip vertical gap is 8. Group-to-group margin is 8 at Medium, 12 at Small.
